prompt: Term life insurance provides:

options.text: Pure death benefit coverage for a specified period without cash value
Guaranteed cash value growth like whole life always
Only disability income benefits
Unlimited policy loans from day one always

explanation: Term is temporary coverage with no savings component.

distractorExplanations.b: Cash value is permanent product feature.
distractorExplanations.d: Term lacks cash value to borrow against.

prompt: Whole life insurance differs from term because whole life:

options.text: Expires after 10 years with no renewal option ever
Combines permanent death benefit with cash value accumulation
Never pays a death benefit
Is always group coverage through an employer only

explanation: Whole life is permanent with level premiums and cash value.

distractorExplanations.a: Term expires; whole life is permanent.

prompt: The beneficiary of a life insurance policy receives:

options.text: Only dividends while insured is alive always
Automatic ownership of the insured's estate without probate rules
The death benefit proceeds upon insured's death
Premium refunds only if insured dies

explanation: Beneficiary is paid death benefit per policy terms.

distractorExplanations.a: Dividends are living benefit feature on participating policies.
distractorExplanations.b: Beneficiary designation avoids probate for proceeds typically.
distractorExplanations.d: Death benefit, not just premium refund.

prompt: Insurable interest must exist between:

options.text: Any stranger with no financial relationship always
Only corporations and governments
The agent and the underwriter exclusively
The policy owner/beneficiary and the insured at policy inception

explanation: Insurable interest prevents wagering on others' lives.

distractorExplanations.a: Strangers generally cannot insure another's life.

prompt: A life insurance policy's incontestability clause generally limits:

options.text: Insurer's ability to contest the policy after it has been in force ~2 years
Premium payments forever
Beneficiary changes after death
All medical underwriting before application

explanation: After contestability period, insurer cannot void except for limited reasons.

prompt: Universal life insurance offers:

options.text: No death benefit component ever
Flexible premiums and adjustable death benefit with cash value earning current crediting rates
Only term coverage expiring at age 65 always
Guaranteed stock market returns without risk

explanation: UL separates insurance cost from cash value with flexibility.

distractorExplanations.d: Crediting varies; not guaranteed equity returns.

prompt: The Human Life Value approach to life insurance need estimates:

options.text: Only funeral costs with no income replacement
Market value of the insured's automobile
Present value of future earnings the family would lose
Social Security tax paid last year only

explanation: HLV quantifies economic loss from premature death.

distractorExplanations.a: Needs approach is broader than funeral only.

prompt: A graded premium whole life policy features:

options.text: Premiums that decrease to zero after one year always
No death benefit until age 100
Only accidental death coverage
Lower initial premiums that increase for a period then level off

explanation: Graded premium helps affordability early with scheduled increases.

prompt: Assignment of a life insurance policy transfers:

options.text: Ownership rights (and sometimes beneficiary) to another party
Only the agent's commission
State insurance department authority
Automatic tax exemption for all transfers

explanation: Absolute assignment transfers ownership; collateral assignment limited.

prompt: Joint life (first-to-die) policy pays when:

options.text: Both insureds die simultaneously only
The first of two insureds dies
Neither insured ever dies
The policy reaches cash value equal to face amount only

explanation: First-to-die covers two lives with benefit at first death.

prompt: Suicide clause in life policies typically:

options.text: Voids all coverage immediately upon any illness
Doubles the death benefit for suicide always
Limits death benefit if suicide occurs within first 1-2 years
Applies only to accidental death riders

explanation: Suicide exclusion period returns premiums or limited benefit early.

distractorExplanations.a: Illness doesn't void like suicide clause.

prompt: A paid-up addition rider uses:

options.text: Loan interest to reduce death benefit only
Surrender charges to fund term coverage exclusively
State guaranty fund contributions
Dividends to purchase additional paid-up whole life insurance

explanation: PUA rider buys extra permanent insurance with dividends.

prompt: Life insurance death benefits paid to named beneficiaries are generally:

options.text: Income tax-free to the beneficiary (estate tax may apply if estate is owner)
Fully taxable as ordinary income always
Subject to capital gains tax as stock sales
Taxed as self-employment income

explanation: IRC §101(a) generally excludes death proceeds from income tax.

prompt: Reduced paid-up insurance nonforfeiture option allows:

options.text: Converting to disability insurance automatically
Stopping premiums while keeping reduced permanent death benefit
Eliminating all coverage with no value
Increasing death benefit without payment

explanation: Reduced paid-up uses cash value to buy smaller paid-up policy.

distractorExplanations.d: Requires no additional premium for increase.

prompt: Second-to-die (survivorship) life insurance pays when:

options.text: The first insured dies only
Premium payments stop
The second of two insureds dies, often used in estate planning
Cash value exceeds $1 million automatically

explanation: Survivorship policy funds estate tax at second death.

prompt: A Health Maintenance Organization (HMO) typically requires:

options.text: No network restrictions ever
Only out-of-network care with highest reimbursement
No premium payments by members ever
Selection of a primary care physician and referrals for specialists

explanation: HMOs emphasize PCP gatekeeping and network care.

prompt: The Affordable Care Act essential health benefits include:

options.text: Ambulatory, emergency, hospitalization, maternity, mental health, prescriptions, and preventive services
Only cosmetic surgery exclusively
Unlimited luxury spa treatments
Only dental for all adults in every plan always

explanation: EHB categories must be covered in individual/small group ACA plans.

prompt: COBRA continuation coverage allows:

options.text: Free lifetime healthcare after any job loss
Former employees to keep group health coverage temporarily by paying full premium
Only Medicare enrollment instead of private coverage
Unlimited duration with no premium increase ever

explanation: COBRA extends group coverage 18-36 months typically at employee cost.

prompt: A Preferred Provider Organization (PPO) differs from HMO by:

options.text: Prohibiting all specialist visits
Requiring only emergency care in-network
Allowing out-of-network care at higher cost sharing without PCP gatekeeper typically
Eliminating all deductibles always

explanation: PPOs offer flexibility with network discounts and OON option.

prompt: Medicare Part A primarily covers:

options.text: Outpatient prescription drugs exclusively
Routine dental and vision for all beneficiaries always
Long-term custodial care unlimited
Inpatient hospital care

explanation: Part A is hospital insurance.

prompt: Medicare Part D provides:

options.text: Prescription drug coverage through private plans
Physician office visits as Part B exclusively
Medigap supplement automatically
Unlimited nursing home care always

explanation: Part D covers outpatient prescription drugs.

prompt: A deductible in health insurance is:

options.text: The agent's commission
Amount the insured pays before plan begins sharing costs
Maximum out-of-pocket always
Premium tax credit from IRS exclusively

explanation: Deductible is initial self-pay threshold.

prompt: Health Savings Accounts (HSAs) offer:

options.text: No requirement to be paired with any insurance
Mandatory use only after age 65 for non-medical without penalty always
Triple tax advantage: deductible contributions, tax-free growth, tax-free qualified medical withdrawals
Unlimited contributions regardless of HDHP enrollment

explanation: HSA tax benefits require qualifying HDHP.

distractorExplanations.b: Non-medical after 65 taxed as income without penalty.

prompt: Pre-existing condition exclusions in individual health insurance post-ACA:

options.text: Are mandatory for all insurers always
Apply only to Medicare Part A
Allow lifetime unlimited exclusions always
Are prohibited for plan years beginning on/after 2014 in compliant markets

explanation: ACA banned pre-existing condition exclusions in individual/small group.

prompt: Stop-loss insurance for self-funded employer plans protects:

options.text: Employer against catastrophic claim levels above specified thresholds
Employees from all copayments always
Only dental claims under $100
Insurance agents from E&O claims exclusively

explanation: Specific and aggregate stop-loss cap employer exposure.

prompt: Medicaid is:

options.text: Identical to Medicare for all seniors automatically
Joint federal-state program for low-income eligible individuals
Private supplemental insurance sold by agents only
Workers compensation exclusively

explanation: Medicaid serves qualifying low-income populations with state variation.

prompt: Coordination of Benefits (COB) determines:

options.text: Only life insurance beneficiary order
Property insurance deductibles exclusively
Which plan pays first when insured has multiple coverages
Agent licensing renewal dates

explanation: COB rules prevent duplicate payment and set primary payer.

prompt: A capitation payment to providers means:

options.text: Fee-for-service for each procedure only always
Payment only after hospital discharge exclusively
Commission to insurance agents per sale
Fixed per-member payment regardless of services rendered

explanation: Capitation shifts utilization risk to provider.

prompt: Long-term care insurance elimination period is similar to:

options.text: A deductible measured in days before benefits begin
Life insurance grace period for premium
Stock market waiting period
Auto collision deductible for property damage only always

explanation: Elimination period is self-pay days before LTC benefits start.

prompt: HIPAA portability provisions primarily:

options.text: Eliminate all health insurance premiums nationally
Limit exclusion periods for pre-existing conditions when changing group coverage
Replace state insurance departments
Mandate universal single-payer healthcare

explanation: HIPAA improved continuity when changing jobs/group plans pre-ACA expansion.

prompt: An insurance contract is considered a contract of:

options.text: Adhesion where only insurer has duties always
Bailment of physical goods exclusively
Utmost good faith requiring full disclosure
Indemnity for all lines without exception always

explanation: Both parties must disclose material facts honestly.

prompt: The principle of indemnity in property insurance means:

options.text: Insured always receives double the loss amount
Insurer never pays claims
Only punitive damages are covered always
Insured should not profit from a loss beyond actual financial loss

explanation: Indemnity restores financial position, not windfall.

prompt: Warranties in insurance contracts differ from representations because warranties:

options.text: Are strictly complied with; breach may void coverage regardless of materiality
Never affect claim payment
Apply only to life insurance dividends
Are optional oral promises only always

explanation: Warranty breach historically voids policy; representations require materiality.

prompt: Agency law: an insurance agent typically represents:

options.text: Always the insured exclusively in all transactions
The insurer when acting as agent of the company
Only the state guaranty fund
No party in any transaction

explanation: Agent of insurer binds principal insurer within authority.

distractorExplanations.a: Broker may represent buyer; agent represents insurer.

prompt: A binder in property-casualty insurance:

options.text: Permanently replaces the policy without conditions
Is only used in life insurance always
Provides temporary coverage until formal policy is issued
Eliminates all underwriting forever

explanation: Binders grant interim protection pending policy issuance.

prompt: The Fair Credit Reporting Act affects insurance by:

options.text: Prohibiting all use of credit in insurance always nationwide
Eliminating privacy rights for consumers
Mandating identical rates for all applicants always
Requiring adverse action notices if credit-based decisions negatively impact consumer

explanation: FCRA governs consumer reports and adverse action disclosure.

distractorExplanations.a: Credit use allowed where permitted with notice.

prompt: Replacement of a life insurance policy requires:

options.text: Disclosure forms comparing existing and proposed coverage per state regulations
No documentation or client acknowledgment ever
Automatic approval by prior insurer always
Only verbal consent from agent

explanation: Replacement regulations protect consumers from unnecessary churn.

prompt: Parol evidence rule in contract law means:

options.text: Oral contracts always override written policies
Written contract terms generally cannot be contradicted by prior oral agreements
Insurance has no written contracts ever
Agents can unilaterally change policy language orally always

explanation: Integrated written policy is primary evidence of agreement.

prompt: Twisting is:

options.text: Legal comparative shopping with full disclosure
Reinsurance between insurers only
Inducing replacement of a policy by misrepresentation or incomplete comparison
Actuarial calculation of mortality

explanation: Twisting is prohibited unfair trade practice.

prompt: Rebating is:

options.text: Paying legitimate policy dividends
Standard agent commission from insurer
Claim payment to beneficiary
Returning premium or giving inducement not specified in the policy as unlawful in most states

explanation: Rebating gives unfair inducement outside policy contract.

prompt: An insurable interest must exist at the time of:

options.text: Application/issue for life; at time of loss for property
Only at claim payment for all lines always
Never; insurance is a wager always allowed
Policy surrender only

explanation: Life: interest at inception; property: at loss (and often at inception).

prompt: The McCarran-Ferguson Act:

options.text: Abolished all state insurance departments
Leaves insurance regulation primarily to states unless federal law specifically applies
Made insurance exclusively a federal program
Prohibited all insurance sales nationally

explanation: McCarran-Ferguson preserves state regulation primacy.

prompt: A certificate of insurance:

options.text: Replaces the entire policy with new terms always
Is only issued by federal courts
Summarizes coverage for a party but does not amend the policy contract
Guarantees unlimited coverage beyond policy limits always

explanation: COI is evidence of coverage; policy governs terms.

prompt: Estoppel may prevent an insurer from denying coverage when:

options.text: Insured intentionally committed fraud always
Premium was never paid and no grace period applied
Loss occurred before policy inception with no binder
Insurer's actions or statements led insured to reasonably rely to their detriment

explanation: Equitable estoppel applies when insurer conduct creates reliance.

prompt: The Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act requires insurers to:

options.text: Provide privacy notices and safeguard nonpublic personal information
Publish all client medical records publicly
Eliminate all encryption requirements always
Share data freely with any vendor without consent always

explanation: GLBA privacy rules apply to financial institutions including insurers.

prompt: Mortality tables used in life insurance pricing reflect:

options.text: Stock market returns exclusively
Statistical death rates by age and sex among insured populations
Only property fire loss frequencies
Agent production quotas

explanation: Actuaries use mortality/morbidity tables for premium calculation.

prompt: Preferred risk classification receives:

options.text: Higher premiums than standard always
Automatic decline of application always
Lower premiums due to favorable health/lifestyle characteristics
No medical underwriting ever required

explanation: Better risk profile earns better rates.

prompt: Attending Physician Statement (APS) in underwriting is:

options.text: The agent's sales presentation only
Proof of property ownership for homeowners
Beneficiary designation form exclusively
Medical records ordered from applicant's doctor for risk assessment

explanation: APS provides detailed medical history from treating physician.

prompt: Substandard (rated) policies are issued when:

options.text: Applicant has health conditions increasing mortality risk above standard
Applicant is in perfect health always
Applicant requests maximum coverage at minimum premium without review
Only group cases qualify always

explanation: Table ratings or flat extras cover extra mortality risk.

prompt: Morbidity tables are used primarily for:

options.text: Life insurance death benefits only always
Disability and health insurance predicting sickness/injury incidence
Auto collision frequency exclusively in all cases
Investment portfolio allocation exclusively

explanation: Morbidity measures illness/disability, not death alone.

prompt: Paramedical exam in life underwriting typically includes:

options.text: Full surgical procedure always
Only credit report with no health data
Height, weight, blood pressure, blood/urine samples
Property inspection of home roof

explanation: Paramed exam is brief physical and fluid collection.

prompt: Anti-selection occurs when:

options.text: Only healthy people buy insurance always
Insurers randomly cancel all policies annually
Agents rebate premiums legally always
Higher-risk individuals seek insurance more than lower-risk individuals

explanation: Adverse selection threatens pool balance without underwriting.

prompt: Reinsurance allows a primary insurer to:

options.text: Transfer portions of risk to another insurer to stabilize capacity
Avoid paying any claims entirely always
Eliminate state regulation completely
Sell policies without licenses always

explanation: Reinsurance spreads catastrophic and large exposures.

prompt: Experience rating in group health modifies premiums based on:

options.text: Only national mortality with no group data ever
The group's own claims experience over time
Agent's personal production only
Stock market index returns exclusively

explanation: Large groups may be experience-rated reflecting utilization.

prompt: Flat extra premium loading adds:

options.text: Only a percentage decrease in death benefit always
Automatic policy cancellation in 30 days always
Fixed dollar amount per $1,000 of coverage for extra hazard
Commission advance to agent exclusively

explanation: Flat extras cover temporary or permanent substandard risks.

prompt: MIB (Medical Information Bureau) report helps underwriters by:

options.text: Guaranteeing approval for all applicants always
Replacing all medical exams permanently
Publishing full medical records publicly
Sharing coded prior insurance application history among member insurers

explanation: MIB flags prior application disclosures for verification.

prompt: Risk classification in auto insurance may use:

options.text: Driving record, territory, vehicle type, and usage
Only applicant's favorite color
Random assignment without actuarial basis always
Only life insurance mortality exclusively

explanation: Auto rates reflect measurable driving and vehicle factors.

prompt: Underwriting declination means:

options.text: Automatic acceptance at standard rates always
Insurer refuses to offer coverage based on risk assessment
Policy issued then immediately surrendered by law
Only temporary coverage through COBRA always

explanation: Decline when risk unacceptable or uninsurable.

prompt: Retention limit for an insurer is:

options.text: Agent's marketing budget exclusively
Beneficiary's share of death benefit always
Maximum loss amount the company keeps before reinsurance applies
State premium tax rate exclusively

explanation: Retention defines self-insured exposure before ceding to reinsurer.

prompt: Conditional receipt in life insurance provides:

options.text: Permanent coverage with no underwriting ever
Only applies after policy has been in force 10 years
Automatic waiver of all premiums forever
Limited temporary coverage if premium paid and conditions met pending underwriting decision

explanation: Conditional receipt may cover if death occurs during underwriting if conditions satisfied.

distractorExplanations.a: Underwriting still required for final issue.
